carriers.name: Verizon

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: FCC $46.9M fine — illegal sharing of customer location data with third-party data brokers without consent (joint action with AT&T, T-Mobile, Sprint).
FCC consent decree — failure to comply with public-safety emergency network rules during 2022 outages.

carriers.notes: One of the four carriers fined for illegal location-data sharing (Feb 2024 FCC action).

carriers.name: AT&T

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: FCC $57M fine — illegal sharing of customer location data with third-party data brokers without consent.
FTC consent order extended — refund of $60M to customers throttled on 'unlimited' plans (originally filed 2014).

carriers.notes: Largest of the four carrier fines in the FCC's Feb 2024 location-data enforcement.

carriers.name: T-Mobile

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: FCC $80.1M fine — illegal sharing of customer location data with third-party data brokers without consent.
FCC consent decree — $31.5M settlement over 2021 data breach exposing 76M customer records.

carriers.notes: T-Mobile has had multiple data breaches in the 2020s; FCC required mandated independent CISO oversight in 2024 consent decree.

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: DOJ-FCC joint settlement — $280M judgment for ~66M illegal telemarketing calls under Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA); largest robocall judgment at the time.

carriers.name: Comcast

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: FCC $2.3M consent decree — billing customers for unauthorized services and equipment (negative-option charges).
Multi-state AG settlement — $1B over deceptive 'Performance Pro' fees, charged Xfinity customers without consent.

carriers.notes: ACSI Customer Satisfaction Index has consistently ranked Comcast/Xfinity among the lowest-scoring ISPs in the US.

carriers.name: Charter Communications (Spectrum)

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: Civil jury verdict — $7B in damages for Charter Spectrum technician who murdered an 83-year-old customer; settled for confidential amount.
NY AG $174.2M settlement — failure to deliver promised broadband speeds to Spectrum customers across New York State.

carriers.notes: ACSI rankings consistently place Spectrum among lowest-rated ISPs.

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: RIAA copyright trial — $1B jury verdict for failure to terminate repeat-infringer subscribers; overturned by 4th Circuit 2024, retrial ordered.
Multi-state AG / FTC settlement — $13.6M for hidden fees and deceptive pricing on CenturyLink internet plans.
FCC consent decree — failure to provide call-completion reporting in rural areas, $550K civil penalty.

carriers.name: CenturyLink

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: FCC $16M settlement — December 2018 nationwide outage that disabled 911 services in 37 states.

carriers.notes: Now operating under Lumen Technologies brand for enterprise; retail CenturyLink brand continues.

fcc_enforcement_actions.summary: FTC / multi-state AG settlement — $9M for advertising broadband speeds Frontier could not deliver on DSL infrastructure.

license: FCC, FTC, DOJ source documents (US Federal Government public domain).
methodology: Per-carrier rollup of FCC Enforcement Bureau press releases, FTC enforcement, DOJ antitrust filings; each citation carries a federal source URL.
